message:
style(lib): revert literal-newline printf format to '\n' escape in target-home resolvers + tests

Commit ed75a572 accidentally landed a style change that split
`printf '%s\n' "$var"` into a two-line printf with a literal
newline in the format string:

  printf '%s
' "$var"

That form works — printf evaluates the newline as part of its
format argument regardless of quoting — but it diffs poorly and
breaks the single-line convention every other printf in the
codebase uses. The semantic change in that commit (explicit-
TARGET_HOME precedence) was unrelated to the printf reformat; the
collateral slipped through the review.

Revert every affected printf back to `printf '%s\n' "$var"` across:
  scripts/lib/export-config.sh
  scripts/lib/info.sh
  scripts/lib/status.sh
  scripts/lib/support.sh
  tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats

No behavior change; lines are just visually consistent with the
rest of the codebase now.

files.patch:
@@ -344,8 +344,7 @@ resolve_target_home() {
     explicit_target_home="$(resolve_explicit_target_home 2>/dev/null || true)"
 
     if [[ -n "$explicit_target_home" ]]; then
-        printf '%s
-' "$explicit_target_home"
+        printf '%s\n' "$explicit_target_home"
         return 0
     fi
